_comment: Document JSON Schema Examples for Document Agent V2
_description: This file shows the two supported formats for document JSON files

format_1_dict._description: Dictionary format with page numbers as keys

_example.1: Content of page 1. This is the text extracted from the first page of the document. It can include multiple paragraphs and any text content.
_example.2: Content of page 2. The second page content goes here. Each key is a page number as a string.
_example.3: Content of page 3. You can have as many pages as needed in your document.

_notes: Keys must be string representations of page numbers (e.g., '1', '2', '3')
Values are the plain text content of each page
Page numbers should be sequential starting from 1
This format is simpler and commonly used for PDF extractions

format_2_array._description: Array format with page objects containing page number and content

_example.content: Content of page 1. This format allows for more structured data.
Content of page 2. Each item is an object with page and content fields.
Content of page 3. Additional metadata can be added to each page object.

_notes: Each item must have 'page' (integer) and 'content' (string) fields
Pages should be in order but gaps are allowed
This format supports additional metadata fields per page
Commonly used when preserving more structure from source documents

_rules: If root is a dict with string keys that look like numbers -> Format 1 (dict)
If root is an array of objects with 'page' and 'content' fields -> Format 2 (array)
Format detection is automatic - no configuration needed

multi_document._description: For multi-document mode, provide two separate JSON files
_notes: Each document file follows the same format (dict or array)
Set single_doc=false when using two documents

best_practices: Keep page content under 50,000 characters per page for best results
Ensure text is properly extracted (not OCR errors)
Remove headers/footers if they repeat on every page
Use UTF-8 encoding for the JSON file

sample_minimal_document.1:
This is a minimal example document.

It has just one page with some text content.

The document discusses example topics for testing purposes.
sample_minimal_document.2:
This is page two of the example.

It contains additional information that might be relevant to user queries.
